original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
original_registry_entry.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

exact_sections.heading: Section 6 — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — management and referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
intended_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
access_limitations: Algorithm or figure references preserved as locators; diagram structure not interpreted
